name: Shark's Cove
tagline: The best shore snorkeling on Oahu — a rocky cove teeming with sea turtles, reef fish, and tide pool life.
parking: Gravel lot across Kamehameha Hwy; fills by 10 AM on weekends
bestTime: Summer (May–Sept) when the water is calm and visibility is 50+ ft; never enter in winter swells
talkStory: Despite the name, no sharks — just incredible snorkeling. The right side has tide pools worth exploring, and the left side drops into deeper water with lava tubes for more advanced snorkelers. Entry over sharp rocks can be tricky — reef shoes are essential. Not ideal for small children due to rocky entry, strong currents on the outer edge, and no sandy beach. Combine with a shave ice run to Ted's Bakery just up the road.
